title: Bead clean-up (single tube)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3hvoe","text":"Protocol for purification of DNA using SPRI beads","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the following reagents/materials: Agencourt AMPure XP Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63880 or Homemade SPRI beads Fresh 70% Ethanol Nuclease-free water or elution buffer DNA sample/PCR product Clean 1.5 mL tubes Magnetic tube rack - Make sure beads are thoroughly mixed/vortexed to ensure they are well resuspended, the solution should be a homogenous brown color. - Beads and sample must be brought to Room temperature before mixing. - Ethanol should be freshly prepared. Due to miscibility of EtOH and water, prepare by adding volumetric parts, not by bringing to volume.
In a 1.5 mL tube, add resuspended beads to sample/PCR product in ratio specified in main protocol. The ratio is dependent on the length of DNA you want to recover.

Allow mixture to incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature Place tube on slow rotation mixer or flick gently periodically throughout the incubation to prevent beads from settling to the bottom of the tube.

Place tube on a magnetic rack. Magnetic Stand (Magnetic Stand, Thermo Scientific, MR02, Any magnetic rack that fits your tubes will suffice., https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/MR02 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9e53sqe.jpeg )
Flick tube gently to mix beads and sample. Pipette mixing may fragment the sample. Do not vortex to mix.
Wait ~1-3 minutes for the beads and buffer to separate. Beads will stick to magnetic side of the tube and the solution should be clear.
Using a pipettor, remove and discard the clear supernatant solution. DNA will remain in the tube bound to the beads.
Add 500 μL 70% EtOH to tube Do not disturb the beads! Pipette into the opposite side of the tube. Volume of 70% EtOH can be adjusted. Just so long as the amount added is enough to cover beads.
Using a pipettor, remove and discard the EtOH. Be careful not to disturb or aspirate the beads.
Repeat the Step 4 EtOH wash. Try to remove as much EtOH as possible without disturbing the beads. A quick spin-down may help to remove residual EtOH.

Leaving tube cap open, allow beads to dry for a maximum of 90 seconds. -Do not over-dry beads. Over-dried beads do not resuspend well and can lead to a loss of DNA. -If the surface of beads appear cracked, they are over-drying. Resuspend immediately.

Remove tube from magnetic rack and resuspend DNA/beads by adding a minimum of 15 μL nuclease-free water or desired buffer. The exact resuspension volume should be specified in your main protocol. Use a smaller volume to increase concentration of DNA
Flick tube gently to resuspend beads in elution.
Place tube back on magnetic rack. Allow beads and elution to separate for 00:01:00 .

Slowly aspirate out clear supernatant, now containing DNA, and transfer to final clean container. Do not carry over any beads. They are a significant inhibitor of various downstream applications. Carefully check your pipette tip for bead carryover. If there are beads, replace the sample into the tube and wait another 2 min. You may need to decrease the volume removed from the tube by 1-2 μL.
creator: Alison K Hamm
